closed_children.title: Phase 1B-1: test infra for XDG-mode integration tests
bl doctor: XDG-mode awareness for check_config + check_state_repo

description:
Phase 1B of bl-f021, sibling of bl-203e (Phase 1A, archived). Filed 2026-05-25 when bl-203e was split; revised 2026-05-25 with deferred-from-1A scope.

## Scope

Per SPEC-clone-layout Â§3, Â§5, Â§6.2, Â§6.4, Â§6.5, Â§6.6, Â§7, Â§11, Â§12.

### Implements

1. **`bl init` rewrite â€” XDG only.**
   - No `.balls/` at the clone root, no `.gitignore` entry, no `balls: initialize` commit on main.
   - Pushes a `balls/tasks` orphan branch with default `repo.json` + `project.json`, no `tracker.json` (solo case).
   - `bl init --stealth [--tasks-dir <path>]` writes `~/.config/balls/<nested-clone-path>/clone.json` with `stealth: true` + `tasks_dir`. No origin requirement; no `trackers/` subtree.
   - Â§14.1, Â§14.19 (no on-main writes in steady state).

2. **`Config` â†’ `EffectiveConfig` migration at the read seam (deferred from 1A).** Phase 1A's XDG-mode `Store::config_path()` puns `repo.json` as if it were the legacy `Config` â€” works accidentally because the layered subset's field names overlap, but breaks the first time an XDG test exercises `state_url`/`target_branch`/`delivery.mode` via the legacy reader. 1B migrates `bl review` / `bl claim` / `bl sync` / `bl close` consumers off `store.load_config()` onto `EffectiveConfig` (the SPEC Â§6.5 three-layer merger that already exists, unused). After this lands, `store.load_config()` either goes away or becomes a legacy-only convenience.

3. **`Â§14.16/17` rename gates â€” `delivery` â†’ `integrate`, `review.pre_check` â†’ `review.gate_command` (deferred from 1A).** Bound to the EffectiveConfig flip above: under Phase 1 dual-read, a legacy `Config` carrying the old field names is read and the renames applied in memory with a one-line warning; the new `RepoJson`/`CloneJson` schemas reject the old names (`deny_unknown_fields`) so a freshly-written file cannot ship the legacy spelling. Conformance tests gated by 1B.

4. **First-contact materialization in `bl init` / `bl prime` (deferred from 1A).** SPEC Â§7 step 4: on a clone whose `~/.local/state/balls/trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/` does not yet exist, single-branch fetch from `origin balls/tasks` into the XDG path. `bl init` does this for a fresh clone; `bl prime` rebuilds it after `rm -rf trackers/` (Â§14.12 full gate, currently partial in 1A). Hard-fail on unreachable explicit tracker (Â§9 carries forward).

5. **bl-fc50 hashed-XDG defense-in-depth (deferred from 1A).** Phase 1A's `xdg_discover` only checks the nested layout. The bl-fc50 SPEC revision never shipped, but defense-in-depth wants `discover` to also probe `~/.local/state/balls/<origin-key>/state-repos/own/.balls/` and fall back through the nested â†’ hashed â†’ pre-XDG preference chain documented in Â§12. Trivial code path; gated by `bl migrate` (Phase 2) to actually convert.

6. **`local_dir` SPEC formalization (deferred from 1A).** Phase 1A invented `~/.local/state/balls/state/<nested>/` as a catch-all for pending-sync queue, `seen-claim-sync-policy` marker, and squash scratch â€” none of which have a SPEC Â§3 home. 1B picks one of:
   - (a) Lobby for an addition to SPEC Â§3 (a 5th per-clone subdir, e.g. `state/<nested>/`).
   - (b) Split each consumer into an existing SPEC dir or `~/.cache/balls/` (the reserved-but-empty cache root).
   - (c) Move pending-sync into `clone.json` (it's per-clone runtime config); move squash scratch to `/tmp/` (it's PID-suffixed and ephemeral); leave the marker file alone in `state/<nested>/` and live with the one-dir SPEC drift.
   The pick lands as a SPEC clarification sub-ball under bl-f021 before 1B's implementation.

7. **Mass migration of init-touching tests.** Each test that called `bl init` now gets XDG paths back; assertions update from `repo.path().join(".balls/...")` to the XDG tracker checkout / per-clone tree. `tests/common/mod.rs` helpers (`read_task_json`, `git_state`, `commit_state_repo`, `push`) gain a layout-aware discovery hook â€” reuse the helpers Phase 1A already shipped in `tests/conformance_xdg_layout.rs` (`seed_tracker_remote`, `fetch_tracker_into_xdg`, `fresh_clone_into`, `bl_xdg`).

### Conformance tests

Â§14.1 (XDG paths from `bl init`), Â§14.4 (bootstrap branch constant â€” bl init writes `origin balls/tasks`, no config field overrides), Â§14.12 (full â€” `rm -rf trackers/` + `bl prime` rebuilds), Â§14.15 (stealth init writes `clone.json`), Â§14.16/17 (rename gates), Â§14.19 (no main commits across full lifecycle â€” half-gated; full gate needs claim XDG too, lands with 1C).

### Explicitly NOT in scope

- `bl claim` worktree relocation â€” Phase 1C (bl-bb6c).
- Tests that don't touch `bl init` â€” Phase 1C.
- `bl migrate` â€” Phase 2 (bl-717e).
- Removing `Store::load_config()` entirely â€” follow-up if all call sites switch cleanly.

## Dependencies

Hard depends on bl-203e (Phase 1A landed). Soft-blocks on the `local_dir` SPEC clarification sub-ball (file before starting #6).
title: Phase 1B: bl init XDG-only flip
